centerDot.title: ● CENTER DOT
centerDot.tooltip: Aiming point at the center of the reticle. Size is set in MRAD and automatically snapped to whole pixels
centerDot.radiusHint: Center dot radius. Value is chosen so the dot occupies a whole number of pixels

wings.title: WINGS
wings.tooltip: A wing is a line from the center dot. Marks are placed along it at equal intervals for measuring distances and corrections
wings.lengthHint:
How far the wing extends from the center dot. 1 MRAD ≈ 10 cm at 100 m, ≈ 1 m at 1 km.

With length {{length}} and interval {{spacing}}, the wing fits {{count}} marks. At 0 the wing is hidden.
wings.lineThicknessHint:
Wing line thickness in MRAD. Thin line (0.05–0.1) covers less of the target, thick (0.2+) is more visible on noisy images.

Value 0 is allowed — no line is drawn, only dot marks remain (if enabled). Useful when you need only a scale without a guide line.
wings.dotSizeHint:
Diameter of each dot mark in pixels. Set directly — without conversion from MRAD. Each wing can have its own size.

1 px — minimum dot, may be hard to see. 2–3 px — clearly visible mark. 4+ px — large, for low resolutions.

Current size {{size}} px = {{mrad}} MRAD on this axis.
wings.intervalHint:
Distance between adjacent dot marks on the wing in MRAD. Determines how frequently the scale divisions are placed.

With interval 1.0 and length {{length}}, there will be {{count}} dots. Mark count = length ÷ interval (fractions are discarded).

This parameter creates the rasterization problem: interval × pixels per MRAD is almost never a whole number of pixels.

scopePanel.title: SCOPE PROFILE
scopePanel.name: Name
scopePanel.nameHint: Name for this parameter set, e.g. "OVOD 640 F50"
scopePanel.typeHint: Digital — scope with electronic display. Optical — with glass reticle
scopePanel.focalLengthHint: Lens focal length. Typical values: 25, 35, 50 mm
scopePanel.displayHintOptical: Resolution for export — output image size
scopePanel.pixelPitchHint: Sensor pixel pitch in micrometers. Typical: 12, 17 µm
scopePanel.fovHint: Scope field of view from manufacturer specifications
scopePanel.nonSquareWarning: ⚠ Non-square pixel ratio — sensor and display proportions don't match

rasterTable.title: ROUNDING TABLE
rasterTable.titleTooltip: Exact coordinates of each mark in pixels and MRAD. Shows where each dot will actually land on the display and how much it deviates from the ideal
rasterTable.summary: {{name}} • {{count}} marks • max error ±{{error}} px • steps: {{steps}} px
rasterTable.colIndexTooltip: Mark number from center. #1 — closest to center, last — farthest on the wing
rasterTable.colMradTooltip: Calculated (ideal) position in milliradians. Formula: mark number × interval. This is where the dot SHOULD be placed
rasterTable.colPxTooltip: Actual position on the scope display in pixels. Whole number — this exact pixel is where the dot will land on export
rasterTable.colActualTooltip: Actual MRAD after rounding to a whole pixel. Formula: pixel position ÷ (pixels per MRAD). Shows the real angular position of the mark
rasterTable.colErrorTooltip: Deviation of actual position from ideal in pixels. Positive (+) — mark shifted away from center. Negative (−) — closer to center. Closer to zero — more accurate
rasterTable.colStepTooltip: Distance in pixels from previous mark to current. Ideally all steps are equal. Orange highlight means the step differs from the standard step for this wing

strategySelector.title: ⚙ ROUNDING
strategySelector.tooltip: How to place dots when MRAD interval doesn't equal a whole number of pixels. Three strategies solve this problem differently
strategySelector.descA:
Each dot is placed on the nearest whole pixel to its ideal position — independently of other dots.

Example: at 1 MRAD = 7.78 pixels, dot #3 should be at pixel 23.34 → placed at pixel 23. Dot #4 at 31.12 → at 31.

✅ Each mark is maximally accurate (error ≤ 0.5 pixel)
✅ Error doesn’t accumulate — 10th mark is as accurate as 1st
⚠️ Distances between adjacent marks may differ by 1 pixel (e.g., alternating steps 8-8-7-8-8-7)

Best option when accuracy of each mark matters for shooting corrections.
strategySelector.descB:
Distance between all dots is the same = nearest whole number to (interval × px/MRAD). For example, if 1 MRAD = 7.78 px, then step = 8 pixels.

Example: dots at 8, 16, 24, 32, 40... pixels. All distances = 8 px.

✅ All steps are equal — easy to document for factory: "step = 8 pixels"
❌ Error accumulates: by 5th mark — already +1.1 px, by 10th — +2.2 px
❌ Distant marks are noticeably shifted from correct positions

Suitable for short wings (3–5 marks) and specs requiring a single step value.
strategySelector.descC:
Alternation of two steps in a predictable pattern. The same principle used in computer graphics for drawing smooth lines from pixels.

Example: at 1 MRAD = 7.78 px → alternating steps of 7 and 8 pixels. Pattern: 8-8-7-8-8-7-8-8-7-8 (repeating cycle).

✅ Error doesn’t accumulate (like A) — each mark is accurate
✅ Visually uniform scale — only two possible step values
✅ Pattern is predictable and documentable

Universal option — best compromise between accuracy and uniformity.
strategySelector.summaryA: Each mark on the nearest pixel to ideal. Error ≤0.5 px, no accumulation, but steps are uneven.
strategySelector.summaryB: All steps identical. Visually flawless, but error accumulates linearly.
strategySelector.summaryC: Identical to A in result. Different implementation (incremental), same result.

strategyComparison.title: Strategy comparison
strategyComparison.colMaxErrorTooltip: Worst mark positioning error across the entire reticle in pixels. Lower is better. Maximum possible rounding error is ±0.5 pixel
strategyComparison.colAvgTooltip: Average error across all marks. Shows typical deviation — how much marks deviate from ideal positions on average
strategyComparison.colFractionalTooltip: How many marks have non-zero error — their ideal pixel position is fractional and was rounded. Format: error count / total. Lower = more accurate for given parameters
strategyComparison.colUnevenStepsTooltip: How many distances between adjacent marks differ from the main step. Strategy B always 0 — all steps equal. A and C have some steps shorter or longer by 1 pixel
strategyComparison.colDriftTooltip: Error of the farthest mark from center. Shows whether error accumulates toward the wing edge. Strategy B error grows with each mark. A and C — no accumulation
strategyComparison.colStepsTooltip: What pixel distances between adjacent marks. "all N" — all equal. "N–M" — two step values alternate
